## 3. Priorities & Struggles

**What matters most (his own words):**
> "Right now, it's only about understanding what's happening, understanding what's worth pursuing and what is not. I have no idea. That's why I'm trying so many things. I need to be at the edge."

The March 9–13 sprint is a deliberate constraint: one question, one week, no scope creep. He's using the sprint to get a real answer about whether David Toolkit has a wedge — not to build more features.

**The deeper orientation (his own words):**
The projects he's building today are not the point. Something he'll start doing seriously in 6 months is the point. Current activity is reconnaissance: what's worth pursuing, what isn't. He's explicitly comfortable not knowing yet.
